event: Ascending infection
Materno-fetal transmission of meningococcus
She was well before delivery
Spontaneous onset of labor
Hospital attendance
Maternal bloods taken 2.5 hours before delivery showed an elevated leucocyte count of 21.1×10⁹/L
Neutrophil count of 18.1×10⁹/L
Fetal distress was noted on the cardiotocography during the second stage of labor
Decision was made to proceed to a ventouse-assisted delivery
Ventouse-assisted delivery
Male infant
25-year-old primigravida mother
Pregnancy was uncomplicated
Birth weight of 3940 grams
Required immediate resuscitation with mask ventilation
The mother remained completely well after birth
No antibiotics were given to the mother
Intubated at 8 minutes of age due to ongoing poor respiratory effort
Transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit (NICU)
Supported with synchronized intermittent positive pressure ventilation (SIPPV) with minimal pressures
No additional oxygen requirement
Blood cultures taken from the newborn pre–antibiotic treatment at 29 minutes of life were negative
Empirical antibiotics were commenced for presumed early-onset sepsis
Gentamicin 4 mg/kg 24-hourly
Benzyl penicillin 60 mg/kg 12-hourly
Heel-prick blood in EDTA taken 5.5 hours postdelivery detected the presence of N. meningitidis genogroup W DNA
Initial full blood counts showed neutropenia
Initial full blood counts showed significant left shift
Respiratory support was weaned
Extubated at 13.5 hours of age
Inflammatory markers were elevated
C-reactive protein (CRP) 60 mg/L
Procalcitonin 68.55 µg/L
Lumbar puncture performed at 23 hours of age
Lumbar puncture revealed normal cell count
Lumbar puncture was negative for a range of pathogens including meningococcus
Placental surface eSwab performed due to clinical concern
eSwab demonstrated light pure growth of an organism
Staining demonstrated Gram-negative diplococci
MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry identified Neisseria meningitidis
Molecular testing confirmed detection of a meningococcus genogroup W clonal complex 11 organism
Contact tracing identified 14 staff members, the 2 parents, and 3 grandparents to be at risk
Chemoprophylaxis with ciprofloxacin was provided
Vaccination with quadrivalent conjugate meningococcal vaccine was provided
Counseling was provided
Benzylpenicillin dosage was decreased back to 60 mg/kg 12-hourly
Changed to cefotaxime 50 mg/kg 12-hourly to complete a 5-day course of β-lactam therapy
Placenta histopathology later revealed acute funisitis
Placenta histopathology revealed chorioamnionitis
The baby was discharged home from the NICU on day 8 of life
